title: I built a deterministic 0DTE SPY options agent in my live brokerage account - no code
author: BuildwithPublic
selftext:
Been experimenting a lot with Agents at r/PublicApp and wanted to share what's possible for active options traders. I honestly never thought in my wildest dreams this tech would be available to retail traders in their brokerage account. 

What I did was I described a simple 0DTE SPY workflow in plain English and the agent built for me and executed it autonomously against my live brokerage account.

**Here's exactly what it does:**

Every weekday at 9:45am ET it scans the SPY 0DTE call chain, filters for contracts with open interest above 1000 and a gamma/theta ratio above 3.0, buys 3 contracts of the top ranked setup, and exits automatically at 50% gain or 30% loss.

What's really interesting from a trader perspective is that you're not just automating execution -you're encoding your actual decision logic into the deterministic workflow. Greeks-based ranking, liquidity filters, risk gates. The agent runs the same process every morning without deviation-purely deterministic, no hallucinations. 

Still super early but this feels like a genuine shift in how active traders will interact with the market. Happy to answer questions on the setup or help you try out agents- DM me 

https://reddit.com/link/1t03g3x/video/92tg7ljp6dyg1/player

comments.author: blkveil
comments.body:
Ran your prompt with ChatGPT and Gemini for feedback: Determine trend before going in, not just going long but also consider puts. Then, OI alone wasn’t good enough. It wanted more along with saying the SL was too rigid for 0DTE which would be hit immediately in wild swings. Also consider not taking trades the closer to noon, etc. interesting takes to say the least. Here’s what Gemini suggested after some brief exchange, let me know what your thoughts are.

\~\~Gemini\~\~

Every weekday at 9:45 AM ET, define the Opening Range by identifying the high and low prices of SPY from the first 15 minutes of the session. Only initiate a trade if the price subsequently breaks and holds above the Opening Range high to buy 0DTE Calls, or breaks and holds below the Opening Range low to buy 0DTE Puts; if the price remains within the range or fails to break out by 11:30 AM ET, do not execute a trade for the day. For a valid breakout, filter the relevant contracts for Open Interest greater than 1,000 and select the strike with the highest Gamma/Theta ratio, proceeding only if that ratio exceeds 3.0. Execute the purchase of 3 contracts using a Limit Order set to the Mid-point price to minimize slippage. Once the trade is live, immediately place a Limit Sell at a +50% profit target and a Stop-Limit at a -35% loss threshold with a 2% offset. If the position is still open at 3:45 PM ET, cancel all pending orders and close the trade at the Market price to avoid final-hour expiration risk.

\~\~ChatGPT\~\~

Configure an automated trading agent to monitor SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust (SPY) 0DTE options each weekday beginning at 9:45 AM ET and only take trades between 9:45 and 11:30 AM ET. First determine directional bias: go long calls only if SPY is trading above VWAP and breaks above the opening range high (first 15 minutes), or go long puts only if SPY is below VWAP and breaks below the opening range low; if no clear breakout occurs, do not trade. Once direction is confirmed, scan the same-day expiration options chain and filter for contracts with delta between 0.40 and 0.60, open interest greater than 1000, volume at least equal to open interest, bid-ask spread of $0.10 or less, and a premium between $0.50 and $3.00. For each candidate, calculate theta as a percentage of price per hour (absolute theta divided by option price) and only consider contracts with theta decay at or below 25% per hour, preferably 20% or less. From the filtered set, select the contract with the highest gamma (or best gamma-to-theta profile among acceptable candidates). Do not enter immediately; instead, wait for a confirmed breakout candle close or a retest and continuation in the breakout direction, then buy 3 contracts using a limit order at the mid-price or better. Manage risk with a hard stop loss at -30% from entry; take profits by selling 2 contracts at +40% and allowing the remaining contract to run with a trailing stop of 20% from peak value or exit if it reaches approximately +80% to +100%. Close any open position by 11:30 AM ET regardless of profit or loss, and skip trades entirely if conditions such as weak volume, wide spreads, excessive theta decay, or choppy price action around VWAP are present.

replies.author: BuildwithPublic
replies.body: Nice love the process here- running it through multiple models for feedback is 100% the right instinct. The ChatGPT version is prob closer to how I'd actually structure it for execution. dual confirmation VWAP + ORB and scale-out on winners are the pieces I'd personally spend time looking at. The 11:30 hard close and Gemini's stop-limit offset are where I'd scrutinize. both get you killed on a true trend day or in a fast moving 0DTE chain with wid-ish spreads.  logic is solid though, the refinement is in the edge cases. What agent setup are you running it through - Claude Desktop, GPT Actions, something else?

replies.author: blkveil
replies.body: I agree with the 11:30am hard stop and understand its logic because you don’t want to get trapped in choppy movement. You also can miss out on a long run but have to be careful with theta decay. Letting it run unattended will force you to monitor the open trades which defeats the purpose behind the agents’ set-and-forget application. I do like the ORB and VWAP confirmation and also the fact that it creates a bidirectional trend seeking agent to take advantage of either trend. I don’t have access to Agents yet so I cannot test this out and see how much complexity it can handle correctly. I’m excited and a little apprehensive getting to test it out and hoping it performs better than the Generated Assets that have not been quite accurate or just time out based on basic instructions. Let me know how it goes.

replies.author: blkveil
replies.body: I’m still on the waiting list unfortunately and I missed the post to get in the last round posted by the COO.
